message:
fix(process): hold ENVIRON_LOCK across fork to avoid child deadlock

REVIEW round 4: fork() ran the user atfork prepare handlers and the
membrane pipeline atfork guard, but did NOT acquire our ENVIRON_LOCK.
If another parent thread was mid-setenv (lock held) at fork time,
the child inherited the held state with a phantom owner. The first
getenv/setenv in the child would then deadlock waiting for a lock
that no thread can ever release in the child's address space.

Mirror the existing pipeline.atfork_prepare pattern: lock
ENVIRON_LOCK before clone, drop the guard in BOTH parent and child
after the syscall returns. parking_lot's lock-state lives inline in
the static, and each side's Drop releases the lock owned by its own
thread.

Drops are also performed in the syscall-failure path so the parent
can resume normal env operations after a failed fork.

Full abi suite: 2976/2976 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
